frase_original: Say not, “I have found the truth,” but rather, “I have found a truth.”Say not, “I have found the path of the soul.” Say rather, “I have met the soul walking upon my path.”For the soul walks upon all paths.The soul walks not upon a line, neither does it grow like a reed.The soul unfolds itself, like a lotus of countless petals.

texto: Life without love is like a tree without blossom and fruit.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kahlil Gibran: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Life without love is like a tree without blossom and fruit.” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, vida, without pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e reflexao.
